displayName: La Flor Dominicana Double Ligero Chiselito Maduro
wrapperRole: Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper sets the top-note character, color, and burn personality for this vitola.
binderRole: Dominican Republic binder supports structure, combustion, and integration with the filler stack.
fillerRole: Dominican Republic filler drives body, strength, and the core flavor arc Sterlon should echo.
flavorNotes: cocoa, earth, pepper, espresso, dark sweetness
avoidIf: Beginner nicotine-sensitive, very short time window, or user wants opposite body/wrapper style.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user asks for 2024 Top 25 picks, Double Ligero, or a full bourbon-friendly smoke at this size.
catalogLine: La Flor Dominicana Double Ligero Chiselito Maduro · 5½ × 54 · Chisel · Full · Dominican · $14 · Notes: cocoa, earth, pepper, espresso
sizeNote: Chiselito format; L4-LFD-DL is full Chisel Double Ligero.

displayName: Drew Estate Liga Privada No. 9 Robusto / Toro / Corona Doble
wrapperRole: Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper delivers dark chocolate, espresso, and fermented sweetness with oily richness.
binderRole: Brazilian Mata Fina binder adds aromatic sweetness and savory depth distinctive to Liga blends.
fillerRole: Honduran and Nicaraguan filler provide earth, subtle barnyard, cocoa, and private-blend density.
flavorNotes: Dark chocolate, espresso, earth, leather, sweet tobacco, cedar, cocoa powder.
bestFor: Celebration, bourbon pairing, private-blend fan, experienced smoker, after-dinner richness.
avoidIf: Beginner, mild Connecticut preference, user dislikes earthy private-blend character.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants benchmark private-blend dark richness with Liga Privada pedigree.
catalogLine: Drew Estate Liga Privada No. 9 Robusto · 5 × 52 · Robusto · Full · Nicaragua · $13 · Notes: dark chocolate, espresso, earth, leather

displayName: Dunbarton Tobacco & Trust Sin Compromiso No. 2 / No. 5 / Seleccion Maduro
wrapperRole: San Andrés maduro wrapper contributes dark earth, cocoa, chicory, and savory sweetness.
binderRole: Ecuador Habano binder adds structure, spice lift, and cohesion for the complex filler stack.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan filler provides body, loam, espresso undertones, and evolving savory depth.
flavorNotes: Hot cocoa, leather, cream, earth, chicory, cedar, subtle sweetness, espresso.
bestFor: Boutique enthusiast, cognac pairing, experienced smoker wanting layered savory-maduro without blunt force.
avoidIf: Beginner, user wants mild Connecticut, or needs lowest price-point daily smoke.
whyRecommend: Recommend for no-compromise boutique balance—rich, savory, and nuanced rather than one-note strength.
catalogLine: Dunbarton Tobacco & Trust Sin Compromiso No. 2 · 6 × 52 · Toro · Medium-Full · Nicaragua · $15 · Notes: hot cocoa, leather, cream, earth
sizeNote: Line also: No. 5, Seleccion — dimensions vary.

displayName: Foundation Cigar Company The Tabernacle CT Broadleaf Robusto / Toro / Lancero
wrapperRole: Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper brings fermented dark sweetness, cocoa, and dense oily texture.
binderRole: Mexican San Andrés binder adds earthy sweetness and savory depth under the broadleaf.
fillerRole: Nicaraguan and Honduran filler supply body, spice, espresso, and a broad dark flavor core.
flavorNotes: Dark cocoa, espresso, leather, earth, molasses, cedar, warming spice.
bestFor: Boutique fan, special occasion, bourbon pairing, user wants broadleaf richness with construction pedigree.
avoidIf: User wants mild cream, short smoke, low nicotine, or very simple Connecticut flavors.
whyRecommend: Recommend when the user wants boutique broadleaf depth with strong identity and texture.
